pattern: The per-length update is a fixed linear map on the up/down counts per value, so raising that transition matrix to the (n-1) power by fast exponentiation handles enormous lengths.
hints: The strict-alternation rule makes the state a vector of up-ending and down-ending counts per value, and each length step applies the same linear transformation.
A fixed linear recurrence over a huge number of steps is the textbook case for matrix exponentiation.

Build the (2m) by (2m) transition matrix where an up-count flows from smaller down-counts and a down-count from larger up-counts, raise it to the n-1 power, apply it to the all-ones start vector, and sum.

edge_inputs.description: large length, small range exercises many squaring bits
odd large length with a tiny range
even moderate length, range of four values

stress.description: moderately large length with small range - matrix power and the DP oracle agree across many bits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, 60000)
    l = rng.randint(1, 70)
    width = rng.randint(1, 5)
    r = l + width
    return [n, l, r]
stress.note: range width <= 5 keeps the O(n*m) oracle fast; n large enough to cover many exponent bits
